Exit with clear error message if context is missing 9→ 10→This eliminates code duplication across kit CLI commands. 11→""" 12→ 13→from __future__ import annotations 14→ 15→from pathlib import Path 16→ 17→import click 18→ 19→from erk_shared.context.context import ErkContext 20→from erk_shared.context.types import LoadedConfig, NoRepoSentinel 21→from erk_shared.core.prompt_executor import PromptExecutor 22→from erk_shared.gateway.branch_manager.abc import BranchManager 23→from erk_shared.gateway.claude_installation.abc import ClaudeInstallation 24→from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import Git 25→from erk_shared.gateway.github.abc import GitHub 26→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.abc import GitHubIssues 27→from erk_shared.gateway.time.abc import Time 28→from erk_shared.plan_store.backend import PlanBackend 29→ 30→

32→ """Get the full ErkContext, exiting with error if not initialized. 35→ initialized (ctx.obj is None), prints error to stderr and exits with code 1. 54→ click.echo("Error: Context not initialized", err=True) 58→ click.echo("Error: Context must be ErkContext", err=True) 65→ """Get GitHub Issues from context, exiting with error if not initialized. 68→ initialized (ctx.obj is None), prints error to stderr and exits with code 1. 87→ click.echo("Error: Context not initialized", err=True) 91→ click.echo("Error: Context must be ErkContext", err=True) 98→ """Get repo root from context, exiting with error if not initialized. 120→ click.echo("Error: Context not initialized", err=True) 124→ click.echo("Error: Context must be ErkContext", err=True) 129→ click.echo("Error: Not in a git repository", err=True) 161→ """Get Git from context, exiting with error if not initialized. 183→ click.echo("Error: Context not initialized", err=True) 190→ """Get GitHub from context, exiting with error if not initialized. 212→ click.echo("Error: Context not initialized", err=True) 219→ """Get current working directory from context, exiting with error if not initialized. 241→ click.echo("Error: Context not initialized", err=True) 248→ """Get ClaudeInstallation from context, exiting with error if not initialized. 269→ click.echo("Error: Context not initialized", err=True) 296→ ... # session.assistant: Phase 5: Update the `/erk:pr-submit` skill.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→description: 3→ Create git commit and submit current branch with Graphite (squashes commits 4→ and rebases stack) 5→argument-hint: <description> 6→context: fork 7→--- 8→ 9→# Submit PR 10→ 11→Automatically create a git commit with a helpful summary message and submit the current branch as a pull request. 12→ 13→**Note:** This command squashes commits and rebases the stack. If you prefer a simpler workflow that preserves your commit history, use `/erk:git-pr-push` instead. 14→ 15→## Usage 16→ 17→```bash 18→# Invoke the command (description argument is optional but recommended) 19→/erk:pr-submit "Add user authentication feature" 20→ 21→# Without argument (will analyze changes automatically) 22→/erk:pr-submit 23→``` 24→ 25→## Implementation 26→ 27→This command delegates to `erk pr submit` which handles everything: 28→ 29→- Preflight checks (auth, squash commits, submit to Graphite) 30→- AI-powered commit message generation
... [38 lines omitted] ...
50→## Error Handling 52→If `erk pr submit` fails, display the error and stop. The Python implementation handles all error cases including: 57→- Submission failures 59→Do NOT attempt to auto-resolve errors. Let the user fix issues and re-run.
session.assistant: Now I need to also check the `erk-diff-analysis` skill since the plan mentions the agent generating the description natively. Let me look at how the agent should generate descriptions.
session.tool_result: Found 2 files .claude/commands/erk/git-pr-push.md .claude/skills/erk-diff-analysis/SKILL.md

1→--- 2→name: erk-diff-analysis 3→description: Internal skill for commit message generation. Only load when explicitly requested by name or invoked by commands. 4→--- 5→ 6→# Diff Analysis 7→ 8→This skill provides the commit message generation prompt used by PR submission commands. 9→ 10→## When to Use 11→ 12→Only load this skill when: 13→ 14→- Explicitly requested by name (`erk-diff-analysis`) 15→- Invoked by commands like `/erk:git-pr-push` 16→ 17→## Usage 18→ 19→Load `references/commit-message-prompt.md` when analyzing a diff, then apply its principles to generate output. 20→ 21→## Key Principles 22→ 23→- Be concise and strategic - focus on significant changes 24→- Use component-level descriptions - reference modules/components, not individual functions 25→- Highlight breaking changes prominently 26→- Note test coverage patterns 27→- Use relative paths from repository root 28→

1→You are a commit message generator. Analyze the provided git diff and return ONLY a commit message. 2→ 3→## Analysis Principles 4→ 5→Analyze the diff following these principles: 6→ 7→- **Be concise and strategic** - focus on significant changes 8→- **Use component-level descriptions** - reference modules/components, not individual functions 9→- **Highlight breaking changes prominently** 10→- **Note test coverage patterns** 11→- **Use relative paths from repository root** 12→ 13→## Level of Detail 14→ 15→- Focus on architectural and component-level impact 16→- Keep "Key Changes" to 3-5 major items 17→- Group related changes together 18→- Skip minor refactoring, formatting, or trivial updates 19→ 20→## Output Format 21→ 22→``` 23→[Clear one-line PR title describing the change] 24→ 25→[2-3 sentence summary explaining what changed and why. State what the branch does (feature/fix/refactor) and highlight key changes briefly.] 26→ 27→## Files Changed 28→ 29→<details> 30→<summary>Files Changed</summary>
... [54 lines omitted] ...
69→[Only if there are breaking changes, security concerns, or important warnings - 1-2 bullets max]

1→"""Centralized test environment helpers for simulating erk scenarios. 2→ 3→This module provides helpers for setting up realistic erk test environments 4→with Click's CliRunner. It provides two patterns: 5→ 6→1. erk_isolated_fs_env(): Filesystem-based (uses isolated_filesystem(), 7→ creates real directories) 8→2. erk_inmem_env(): In-memory (uses fakes only, no filesystem I/O) 9→ 10→Key Components: 11→ - ErkIsolatedFsEnv: Helper class for filesystem-based testing 12→ - ErkInMemEnv: Helper class for in-memory testing 13→ - erk_isolated_fs_env(): Context manager for filesystem-based tests 14→ - erk_inmem_env(): Context manager for in-memory tests 15→ 16→Usage Pattern: 17→ 18→ Before (raw isolated_filesystem pattern - 20-30 lines per test): 19→ ```python 20→ def test_something() -> None: 21→ runner = CliRunner() 22→ with runner.isolated_filesystem(): 23→ cwd = Path.cwd() 24→ git_dir = cwd / ".git" 25→ git_dir.mkdir() 26→ erk_root = cwd / "erks" 27→ erk_root.mkdir() 28→ 29→ git = FakeGit(git_common_dirs={cwd: git_dir}) 30→ global_config = GlobalConfig.test(...)

57→ # Test command failure 61→ ... except RuntimeError: 62→ ... print("Command failed as expected") 82→ simulated_process_error: str | None = None, 84→ simulated_prompt_error: str | None = None, 92→ command_should_fail: Whether execute_command should raise RuntimeError 101→ simulated_process_error: Error message to simulate process startup failure 104→ simulated_prompt_error: Error message to return from execute_prompt() on failure. 105→ If set, execute_prompt() will return a failure result. 108→ tests the "empty response" failure mode where Claude produces JSON 122→ self._simulated_process_error = simulated_process_error 124→ self._simulated_prompt_error = simulated_prompt_error 167→ RuntimeError: If command_should_fail was set to True 171→ # Process error takes precedence (simulates Popen failure) 172→ if self._simulated_process_error is not None: 173→ yield ProcessErrorEvent(message=self._simulated_process_error) 187→ diag += "\n Run 'claude' directly to see hook error messages" 193→ yield ErrorEvent(message=f"Claude command {command} failed (simulated failure)") 251→ RuntimeError: If command_should_fail was set to True 263→ error_message=f"Claude command {command} failed (simulated failure)", 274→ error_message=None, 295→ RuntimeError: If Claude CLI is not available 298→ raise RuntimeError("Claude CLI not found\nInstall from: https://claude.com/download") 352→ PromptResult with simulated output or error 356→ if self._simulated_prompt_error is not None: 360→ error=self._simulated_prompt_error, 371→ error=None,
